nodes.html:
Haikus of the Forest is a digital photo essay and shader project that explores mindfulness. A series of natural forest landscapes are rendered through a custom WebGL fragment shader.

Initially, the images are highly pixelated and blurred, representing the overwhelming fog of anxiety and self-doubt. As the viewer scrolls, the custom code shader gradually resolves the image into a sharp, focused landscape of trees. Alongside the visual resolution, short, nature-inspired haikus slowly fade onto the canvas, mirroring the clarity and peace of mind that comes from stepping away from screens and finding poetic focus in nature.

nodes.html:
Generative Poetry Canvas is an interactive, web-based digital art piece exploring the transition from cognitive chaos to semantic order. The page initially displays a swarm of dark, floating words—common imposter phrases and negative self-talk—that drift and clash aimlessly.

When a user clicks and draws on the canvas, they create paths of light that act as structural gravitational guidelines. The floating words are drawn to these lines, aligning themselves into neat, legible stanzas of poetry. By interacting with the canvas and shifting control parameters, the user actively reframes random mental noise into a beautiful, coherent lyric, acting as a metaphor for creative self-authorship.

nodes.html:
The Poetry Shield is a visual art series of hand-pulled screenprints and expressive charcoal overlaying on heavy raw canvas. These pieces display stylized book spines stacked to form defensive shields, symbolizing the cognitive barrier that literature and poetry provide.

Adorning the margins are hand-written stanzas of therapeutic poems. Cruel, critical phrases like 'fraud' and 'cheat' are depicted as arrows and spears that physically bounce off the screenprinted book canopies. The dark charcoal captures the heavy emotional texture of self-doubt, while the colored inks of the book titles represent the protective light of self-acceptance.

nodes.html:
Inspired by the traditional Japanese practice of Kintsugi—mending broken pottery with golden lacquer—Stanzas of Clay is a series of open ceramic book sculptures. Etched into the unfired clay are raw, scratchy diary entries and freeform poems detailing the dread of being an imposter.

The clay books are intentionally fractured during the firing process, and the resulting cracks are meticulously filled with gold leaf. This visual metaphor highlights that our vulnerabilities, anxieties, and mistakes are not things to be hidden or erased. Instead, they are unique, golden seams of our history that make our creative voices more authentic.

nodes.html:
The Whispering Inflatable is an interactive installation that translates emotional externalization into a physical experience. Visitors enter a dimly lit room containing a massive, towering latex structure that gently sighs and inflates, crowding the physical space.

To interact with the sculpture, visitors stand before a microphone and read lines of therapeutic or self-reflective poetry. When sound sensors detect the rhythmic reading and vocal pitch of the poetry, pneumatic valves trigger, releasing air from the structure. The more poetry is read, the more the towering inflatable deflates, eventually shrinking into a small, harmless heap on the floor. This tactile feedback loop shows how expressing our internal critic through language strips it of its intimidating size.

nodes.html:
Echoes of the Lyric is a sensory audio installation exploring isolation and shared understanding. The gallery space is kept entirely dark, save for three circular pools of light cast by overhead spotlights onto the floor.

In the dark zones, directional dome speakers play a low, overlapping soundscape of whispers representing imposter doubts ("You don't belong here," "You're going to fail"). However, once a visitor steps directly into one of the bright spotlights, the whispering audio cuts out. In its place, the speaker plays clear, intimate recordings of spoken-word poetry about self-acceptance and creative resilience. The transition mimics the shift from isolated, dark overthinking to the light of creative expression and shared truth.
